key_insight: Agentic Symbolic Search (ASYS) uses prior-guided symbolic program search plus gradient-based parameter fitting to recover or discover interpretable PDE formulas beyond meshes and neural nets.
engineering_relevance: The method can produce compact, human-readable surrogate laws for complex PDE dynamics, potentially accelerating analysis, debugging, and theory development in scientific computing workflows.
method: Agent translates PDE theory, problem constraints, and prior search experience into differentiable symbolic programs.
Evolutionary search explores mathematical forms while gradients optimize continuous parameters.
Acts as inductive-bias injection for symbolic discovery rather than blind symbolic regression.
evidence_signal: Across five benchmark problems spanning bounded dynamics, finite-time blow-up, and free-boundary focusing, ASYS recovered known forms and found new interpretable approximations such as an Allen-Cahn interface formula and a nine-parameter Keller-Segel blow-up law.
watch_item: The approach depends on high-quality priors, constraint encoding, and search design, so discovered formulas may be sensitive to problem setup and require expert validation.
paper_title: Agentic Symbolic Search: Characterizing PDEs Beyond Hand-crafted Expressions, Meshes, and Neural Networks
